verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have given them Thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When having become virtuous we are persecuted by the wicked, or when being desirous of virtue we are mocked at by them, let us not be distracted or angry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the natural course of things, and everywhere virtue is wont to engender hatred from wicked men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For envying those who desire to live properly, and thinking to prepare an excuse for themselves if they can overthrow the credit of others, they hate them as having pursuits opposite to their own, and use every means to shame their way of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let not us grieve, for this is a mark of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ also saith, “If ye were of the world, the world would love its own.” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in another place again, “Woe unto you when all men shall speak well of you.” ( Luke vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also He saith here, “I have given them Thy word, and the world hath hated them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He telleth the reason for which they were worthy to obtain much care from the Father; “For Thy sake,” He saith, “they have been hated, and for Thy word’s sake”; so that they would be entitled to all providential care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I pray not that Thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that Thou shouldest keep them from the evil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He simplifieth His language; again He rendereth it more clear; which is the act of one showing, by making entreaty for them with exactness, nothing else but this, that He hath a very tender care for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He Himself had told them, that the Father would do all things whatsoever they should ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth He here pray for them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As I said, for no other purpose than to show His love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith He in another place, “Which Thou gavest Me out of the world; Thine they were”? ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There He speaketh of their nature; here of wicked actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He putteth together a long encomium of them; first, that “they were not of the world”; then, that “the Father Himself had given them”; and that “they had kept His word;” and that on this account “they were hated.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if He saith, “As I am not of the world,” be not troubled; for the “as” is not here expressive of unvarying exactness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as, when in the case of Him and the Father the “as” is used, a great Equality is signified, because of the Relationship in Nature; so when it is used of us and Him, the interval is great, because of the great and infinite interval between the respective natures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if He “did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth” ( 1 Pet. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 22 ), how could the Apostles be reckoned equal to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is it then that He saith, “They are not of the world”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They look to another world, they have nothing common with earth, but are become citizens of heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by these words He showeth His love, when He commendeth them to the Father, and committeth them to Him who begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He saith, “Keep them,” He doth not speak merely of delivering them from dangers, but also with regard to their continuance in the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Sanctify them through Thy truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Make them holy by the gift of the Spirit, and of right doctrines.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As when He saith, “Ye are clean through the word which I spake unto you” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ), so now He saith the same thing, “Instruct them, teach them the truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And yet He saith that the Spirit doth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth He now ask it from the Father?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest again learn their equality of Honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For right doctrines asserted concerning God sanctify the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if He saith that they are sanctified by the word, marvel not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show that He speaketh of doctrines, He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thy word is truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “there is no falsehood in it, and all that is said in it must needs come to pass”; and again, it signifieth nothing typical or bodily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As also Paul saith concerning the Church, that He hath sanctified it by the Word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Word of God is wont also to cleanse. ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, the, “sanctify them,” seems to me to signify something else, such as this, “Set them apart for the Word and for preaching.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is made plain from what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As Thou hast sent Me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As Paul also saith, “Having put in us the word of reconciliation.” ( 2 Cor. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the same end for which Christ came, for the same did these take possession of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this place again the “as” is not put to signify resemblance in the case of Himself and the Apostles; for how was it possible for men to be sent otherwise?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it was His custom to speak of the future as having come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And for their sakes I sanctify Myself, that they also might be sanctified in the truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is, “I sanctify Myself”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I offer to Thee a sacrifice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now all sacrifices are called “holy,” and those are specially called “holy things,” which are laid up for God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For whereas of old in type the sanctification was by the sheep, but now it is not in type, but by the truth itself, He therefore saith, “That they may be sanctified in Thy truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For I both dedicate them to Thee, and make them an offering”; this He saith, either because their Head was being made so, or because they also were sacrificed; for, “Present,” it saith, “your bodies a living sacrifice, holy” ( Rom. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ); and, “We were counted as sheep for the slaughter.” ( Ps. xliv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He maketh them; without death, a sacrifice and offering; for that He alluded to His own sacrifice, when He said, “I sanctify,” is clear from what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also who shall believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He was dying for them, and said, that “For their sakes I sanctify Myself,” lest any one should think that He did this for the Apostles only, He added, “Neither pray I for these only, but for them also who believe on Me through their word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this again He revived their souls, showing that the disciples should be many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because He made common what they possessed peculiarly, He comforteth them by showing that they were being made the cause of the salvation of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After having thus spoken concerning their salvation, and their being sanctified by faith and the Sacrifice, He afterwards speaketh of concord, and finally closeth his discourse with this, having begun with it and ended in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For at the beginning He saith, “A new commandment I give unto you” ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 34 ); and here,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That they all may be one, as Thou, Father, art in Me and I in Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again the “as” doth not denote exact similarity in their case, (for it was not possible for them in so great a degree,) but only as far as was possible for men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Just as when He saith “Be ye merciful, as your Father.” ( Luke vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “In Us”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the faith which is on Us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because nothing so offends all men as divisions, He provideth that they should be one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then,” saith some one, “did He effect this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Certainly He effected it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all who believe through the Apostles are one, though some from among them were torn away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor did this escape His knowledge, He even foretold it, and showed that it proceeded from men’s slack-mindedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That the world may believe that Thou hast sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As He said in the beginning, “By this shall all men know that ye are My disciples, if ye love one another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how should they hence believe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because,” He saith, “Thou art a God of peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If therefore they observe the same as those of whom they have learnt, their hearers shall know the teacher by the disciples, but if they quarrel, men shall deny that they are the disciples of a God of peace, and will not allow that I, not being peaceable, have been sent from Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how, unto the end, He proveth His unanimity with the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the glory which Thou gavest Me, I have given them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That by miracles, that by doctrines, and, that they should be of one soul; for this is glory, that they should be one, and greater even than miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As men admire God because there is no strife or discord in That Nature, and this is His greatest glory, “so too let these,” He saith, “from this cause become glorious.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how,” saith some one, “doth He ask the Father to give this to them, when He saith that He Himself giveth it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whether His discourse be concerning miracles, or unanimity, or peace, He is seen Himself to have given these things to them; whence it is clear that the petition is made for the sake of their comfort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I in them, and Thou in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How gave He the glory?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By being in them, and having the Father with Him, so as to weld them together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in another place He speaketh not so; He saith not that the Father cometh by Him, but, “that He and the Father come, and take up their abode with him,” “there” removing the suspicion of Sabellius, “here” that of Arius.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That they may be made perfect in one, and that the world may know that Thou hast sent Me.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith these latter words immediately after the other, to show that peace hath more power to attract men than a miracle; for as it is the nature of strife to separate, so it is that of agreement to weld together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I have loved them as Thou hast loved Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again the “as” means, as far as it is possible for a man to be loved; and the sure proof of His love is His giving Himself for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After having told them that they shall be in safety, that they shall not be overturned, that they shall be holy, that many shall believe through them, that they shall enjoy great glory, that not He alone loved them, but the Father also; He next telleth them of what shall be after their sojourning here, concerning the prizes and crowns laid up for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Father,” He saith, “I will that they also whom Thou hast given Me, be with Me where I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then dost Thou gain by prayer, and dost Thou not yet possess that concerning which they enquired continually, saying, ‘Whither goest Thou?’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What sayest Thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then didst Thou say to them, ‘Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones’? ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How didst Thou promise other things more and greater?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He saith all in the way of condescension? since how would He have said, “Thou shalt follow afterwards”? ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He speaketh thus with a view to a fuller conviction and demonstration of His love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That they may behold My glory which Thou hast given Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This again is a sign of His being of one mind with the Father, of a higher character than those former, for He saith, “Before the foundation of the world,” yet hath it also a certain condescension; for, “Thou hast given Me,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if this be not the case, I would gladly ask the gainsayers a question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that giveth, giveth to one subsisting; did the Father then, having first begotten the Son, afterwards give Him glory, having before allowed Him to be without glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how could this be reasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest that the “He gave,” is, “He begot”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why said He not, “That they may share My glory,” instead of, “That they may be hold My glory”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He implieth, that all that rest is, the looking on the Son of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This certainly it is which causes them to be glorified; as Paul saith, “With open face mirroring the glory of the Lord.” ( 2 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as they who look on the sunbeams, and enjoy a very clear atmosphere, draw their enjoyment from their sight, so then also, and in much greater degree, this will cause us pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the same time also He showeth, that what they should behold was not the body then seen, but some awful Substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “O righteous Father, the world hath not known Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What connection hath it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He here showeth that no man knoweth God, save those only who have come to know the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what He saith is of this kind: “I wished all to be so, yet they have not known Thee, although they had no complaint against Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the meaning of, “O righteous Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here He seemeth to me to speak these words, as vexed that they would not know One so just and good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since the Jews had said that they knew God, but that He knew Him not, at this He aimeth, saying, “For Thou lovedst Me before the foundation of the world”; thus putting together a defense against the accusations of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how could He who had received glory, who was loved before the foundation of the world, who desired to have them as witnesses of that glory, how could He be opposed to the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This then is not true which the Jews say, that they know Thee, and that I know Thee not; on the contrary, I know Thee, and they have not known Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And these have known that Thou hast sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He alludeth to those, who said that He was not from God, and all is finally summed up to meet this argument?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I have declared unto them Thy Name, and will declare it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet thou sayest that perfect knowledge is from the Spirit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But the things of the Spirit are Mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That the love wherewith Thou hast loved Me may remain in them, and I in them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if they learn who Thou art, then they shall know that I am not separated from Thee, but one of the greatly beloved, and a true Son, and closely knit to Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And those who are rightly persuaded of this, will keep both the faith which is on Me and perfect love; and while they love as they ought, I remain in them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He hath arrived at a good end, finishing off the discourse with love, the mother of all blessings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then believe and love God, that it may not be said of us, “They profess that they know God, but in their works they deny Him.” ( Tit. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “He hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.” ( 1 Tim. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he helps his domestics and kinsmen and strangers, while thou dost not even succor those who are related to thee by family, what will henceforth be thy excuse, when God is blasphemed and insulted by reason of thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider what opportunities of doing good God hath given to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Have mercy on one,” He saith, “as a kinsman, on another as a friend, on another as a neighbor, on another as a citizen, on another as a man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if none of these things hold thee, but thou breakest through all bonds, hear from Paul, that thou art “worse than an infidel”; for he having heard nothing of almsgiving, or of heavenly things, hath overshot thee in love for man; but thou who art bidden to love thy very enemies, lookest upon thy friends as enemies, and art more careful of thy money than of their bodies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the money by being spent will sustain no injury, but thy brother if neglected will perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What madness then to be careful of money, and careless about one’s kindred?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence hath this craving for riches burst in upon us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence this inhumanity and cruelty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if any one could, as though seated on the highest bench of a theater, look down upon all the world,—or rather, if you will, let us for the present take in hand a single city,—if then a man seated on an elevated spot could take in at a glance all the doings of the men there, consider what folly he would condemn, what tears he would weep, what laughter he would laugh, with what hatred he would hate; for we commit such actions as deserve both laughter, and the charge of folly, and tears, and hatred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: One man keeps dogs to catch brute animals, himself sinking into brutality; another keeps oxen and asses to transport stones, but neglects men wasting with hunger; and spends gold without limit to make men of stone, but neglects real men, who are becoming like stones through their evil state.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Another, collecting with great pains golden quarries, puts them about his walls, but when he beholds the naked bellies of the poor, is not moved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some again contrive garments over their very garments, while their brother hath not even wherewithal to cover his naked body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, one hath swallowed up another in the law-courts; another hath spent his money on women and parasites, another on stage-players and theatrical bands, another on splendid edifices, on purchases of fields and houses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, one man is counting interest, another interest of interest; another is putting together bands full of many deaths, and doth not enjoy rest even at night, lying awake for others’ harm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, when it is day, they run, one to his unjust gain, another to his wanton expense, others to public robbery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And great is the earnestness about things superfluous and forbidden, but of things necessary no account is taken; and they who decide questions of law have indeed the name of jurymen, but are really thieves and murderers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if one should enquire into law suits and wills, he would find there again ten thousand mischiefs, frauds, robberies, plots, and about these things is all time spent; but for spiritual things there is no care, and they all inconvenience the Church, for the sake of seeing only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this is not what is required; we need works, and a pure mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou spendest all the day in grasping after riches, and then coming in sayest a few words, thou hast not only not propitiated God, but hast even angered Him more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou conciliate thy Lord, exhibit works, make thyself acquainted with the mass of woes, look upon the naked, the hungry, the wronged; He hath cut out for thee ten thousand ways of showing love for men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then deceive ourselves by living aimlessly and to no purpose, nor presume, because we now are in health; but bearing in mind, that often when we have fallen into sickness, and have reached the extreme of debility, we have been dead with fear and the looking for things to come, let us expect to fall again into the same state, let us get again the same fear, and let us become better men; since what is done now deserves infinite condemnation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those in the courts of justice are like lions and dogs; those in the public places like foxes; and those who lead a life of leisure, even they do not use their leisure as they ought, spending all their time on theaters and the mischiefs arising from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And there is no one to reprove what is being done; but there are many who envy, and are vexed that they are not in the like condition, so that these in their turn are punished, though not actually doing wicked things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they “not only do these things, but also have pleasure in them that do them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because what belongs to their will is alike corrupt; whence it is plain, that the intention also will be punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things I say each day, and I will not cease to say them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if any listen, it is gain; but if none give heed, ye shall then hear these things, when it will avail you nothing, and ye shall blame yourselves, and we shall be flee from fault.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But may it never come to pass that we should only have this excuse, but that you may be our boast before the judgment-seat of Christ, that together we may enjoy the blessings, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, for ever and ever.
